content:
# Ephesians Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/EPH_001_page_001.jpg, EPH_001_page_002.jpg

1. Paulos, an apostle of Christos Yeshua through [the] will of God, to the holy [ones] who are in Ephesos and faithful in Christos Yeshua:

2. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and [the] Lord Yeshua Christos.

3. Blessed [be] the God and Father of our Lord Yeshua Christos, the [one] having blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly [places] in Christos,

4. just as he chose us in him before [the] foundation of [the] world, for us to be holy and blameless before him in love,

5. having predestined us unto adoption as sons through Yeshua Christos to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,

6. unto [the] praise of [the] glory of his grace, with which he graced us in the Beloved.

7. In whom we have the redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of the trespasses, according to the riches of his grace,

8. which he caused to abound toward us in all wisdom and understanding,

9. having made known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he purposed in him,

10. unto [the] administration of the fullness of the times, to sum up all things in the Christos — the [things] in the heavens and the [things] upon the earth — in him,

11. in whom also we were allotted [an inheritance], having been predestined according to [the] purpose of the [one] working all things according to the counsel of his will,

12. that we should be unto [the] praise of his glory, the [ones] having previously hoped in the Christos.

13. In whom also you, having heard the word of the truth, the gospel of your salvation — in whom also having believed, you were sealed with the Holy Spirit of the promise,

14. who is [the] pledge of our inheritance, unto [the] redemption of the possession, unto [the] praise of his glory.

15. Because of this, I also, having heard of your faith in the Lord Yeshua and your love toward all the holy [ones],

16. do not cease giving thanks for you, making mention in my prayers,

17. that the God of our Lord Yeshua Christos, the Father of the glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and of revelation in [the] knowledge of him,

18. the eyes of your heart having been enlightened, that you may know what is the hope of his calling, what [are] the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the holy [ones],

19. and what [is] the surpassing greatness of his power toward us the [ones] believing, according to the working of the might of his strength,

20. which he worked in the Christos, having raised him from [the] dead and having seated [him] at his right [hand] in the heavenly [places],

21. far above every ruler and authority and power and lordship and every name being named, not only in this age but also in the coming [age],

22. and he subjected all things under his feet and gave him [as] head over all things to the assembly,

23. which is his body, the fullness of the [one] filling all things in all.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "in Ephesos":** The words "in Ephesos" (en Ephesō) are present in Sinaiticus but absent from some early manuscripts (P46, original hand of Vaticanus). This has led to the theory that Ephesians was a circular letter with a blank space for the city name.

2. **v.3-14 — One sentence:** In Greek, vv.3-14 constitute a single sentence — the longest in the NT (202 words in Greek). This is a berakah (blessing) modeled on Jewish liturgical prayer, cascading through the work of Father (3-6), Son (7-12), and Spirit (13-14).

3. **v.6 — "he graced us":** echaritōsen hēmas — a verb cognate with charis (grace). "He graced us with grace" — the cognate construction emphasizes grace as both the act and the gift.

4. **v.10 — "to sum up all things":** anakephalaiōsasthai ta panta — "to recapitulate/bring under one head." The verb is from kephalē (head) — God's plan is to bring all things under Christ as head. This cosmic Christology is central to Ephesians.

5. **v.13 — "sealed with the Holy Spirit":** esphragisthēte tō pneumati — the seal (sphragis) was a mark of ownership and authentication in the ancient world. The Spirit is the seal — the mark that believers belong to God.

6. **v.14 — "pledge":** arrabōn — a commercial term: a down payment or earnest money guaranteeing the full payment. The Spirit is the first installment of the eschatological inheritance.

7. **v.23 — "the fullness of the one filling all in all":** to plērōma tou ta panta en pasin plēroumenou — the most compressed christological statement in Ephesians. The church is Christ's fullness, and Christ fills all things in every way.

— EMET, AI Translator
